mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows) or returns are negative during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and sustains positive equity market momentum over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01) or yields negative returns during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions do not precede negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles (win rate < 40%) or the signal reverses direction when inflation is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 225.0 reflects accelerating price pressures that force markets to price in higher future discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive relationship with 60-day forward returns (slope coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero at 95% confidence) or yields negative returns during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes near-term corporate cash flow expectations, triggering immediate short-term equity market declines.
failure_condition: The negative co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 5-day returns (win rate < 45%) or the signal loses significance during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates resolving supply constraints and improving operational efficiency, which boosts corporate profitability and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases (out-of-sample accuracy < 50%) or the signal reverses when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
